design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a Disney theme park attraction from overworld hub, complete stage to collect silver keys, return to hub to select next attraction or attempt trivia quest
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Collecting six silver keys through varied stage types with different mechanical demands; trivia knowledge barrier as alternative path
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Explore Disneyland-like theme park, interact with Disney characters, and unlock the castle parade by solving Disney-themed challenges
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hub-and-spoke structure with five mechanically distinct stage types (top-down driving, top-down rail, side-scrolling action, first-person shooting, trivia) creates a novelty-driven design prioritizing variety over depth. Deliberately asymmetrical difficulty across stages suggests design aimed at broad accessibility rather than mastery

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose which of five stages to attempt in any order, suggesting ability to route around difficult stages. Trivia quest provides knowledge-based alternative path to progression

progression.unlock_mechanism: Collect six silver keys by completing five different themed stages plus trivia challenge. Final gate opens after all six keys collected, triggering parade ending sequence
replayability_hooks: Five distinct stage types encourage replaying to master different mechanics
Disney trivia quiz with multiple questions supports repeat playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Theme park hub layout mirrors real Disneyland/Magic Kingdom/Tokyo Disneyland geography
NPC dialogue from children asking Disney trivia
Stage theming tied to actual Disney attractions (Haunted Mansion, Pirates of the Caribbean, Space Mountain, Big Thunder Mountain Railroad, Autopia)
Mickey Mouse and Goofy establish quest motivation
Cinderella Castle as central unlock objective

notable_bosses: Panhandle Pete (Autopia stage antagonist in racing challenge)
Pirates (Pirates of the Caribbean stage enemies requiring rescue of six villagers)
Ghosts (Haunted Mansion stage defeated by throwing candles)
Enemy ships (Space Mountain first-person shooting stage)

faction_indicators: Disney character alignment (heroes: Mickey, Goofy; neutral: NPCs asking trivia; villains: Panhandle Pete, pirates, ghosts)
danger_signals: Pirates raiding island (Pirates of the Caribbean narrative framing)
Asteroid field and enemy ships in Space Mountain
environmental_storytelling: Disneyland theme park structure communicates safe exploration space
Individual attraction theming (graveyard in Haunted Mansion, pirate village in Caribbean stage) establishes stage identity
Castle gate as central landmark reinforces main objective
NPC children scattered around park suggest community and safety
Unused graphics evidence (Madame Leota's crystal ball, ornate furnishings) indicate abandoned supernatural worldbuilding depth
